gene_region.label: TERF1 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical TERF1 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: TERF1 encodes telomeric repeat-binding factor 1, a shelterin component regulating telomere length and replication. This topic bundle emphasizes aging and cellular maintenance, with interpretation centered on telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local TERF1 bundle is oriented toward aging and cellular maintenance. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: TERF1 variants can alter, tag, or contextualize telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Aging, longevity, and cellular maintenance research context
Telomere biology, DNA repair, oxidative stress, and senescence pathways
Progeroid, degenerative, cancer-risk, and stress-resilience review
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: TERF1 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for TERF1.
Because TERF1 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use TERF1 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
Aging, longevity, and cellular maintenance research context
Telomere biology, DNA repair, oxidative stress, and senescence pathways

biorender_visuals.label: TERF1 biochemical figure starter

biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a TERF1 aging and cellular maintenance thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_records.common_name: TERF1 aging and cellular maintenance pathway model
variant_records.clinical_interpretation: TERF1 aging-maintenance variation model is curated as a TERF1 marker for Aging, longevity, and cellular maintenance research context, Telomere biology, DNA repair, oxidative stress, and senescence pathways. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
